content.text: I'd be happy to create a PRD, but I need to know what it's for. "Create a PRD" on its own doesn't tell me the product, audience, or scope. Let me get the essentials.

questions.question: What product or feature is this PRD for?

options.label: A new product
options.description: A net-new product or app being built from scratch

options.label: A feature/enhancement
options.description: A new capability added to an existing product

options.label: A technical/internal tool
options.description: An internal service, API, CLI, or developer tool

questions.question: What format and depth do you want?

options.label: Lean one-pager
options.description: Problem, goals, scope, success metrics — fits on a page

options.label: Standard PRD
options.description: Full doc: context, requirements, user stories, metrics, risks, timeline

options.label: Detailed spec
options.description: Comprehensive with edge cases, API contracts, acceptance criteria

content.text: You didn't specify a product, so I'll do the most useful thing I can without guessing: create a clean, reusable **standard PRD template** as a markdown file, with inline guidance you can fill in. You can tell me the actual product anytime and I'll turn this into a filled-out doc.

input.content:
# Product Requirements Document — <Product / Feature Name>

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Author** | <name> |
| **Status** | Draft · In Review · Approved |
| **Last updated** | 2026-06-05 |
| **Stakeholders** | <PM, Eng lead, Design, etc.> |
| **Target release** | <version / date> |

---

## 1. Summary
> One paragraph: what we're building and why, in plain language. Someone should grasp the whole thing from this section alone.

## 2. Problem & Context
- **Problem statement** — What user/business pain are we solving?
- **Evidence** — Data, research, or feedback that proves this is worth doing.
- **Why now** — What makes this the right time?

## 3. Goals & Non-Goals
**Goals**
- <Measurable outcome 1>
- <Measurable outcome 2>

**Non-Goals** *(explicitly out of scope — prevents scope creep)*
- <Thing we are deliberately not doing>

## 4. Target Users & Use Cases
- **Primary persona** — Who they are, what they need.
- **Key use cases / user stories**
  - As a `<user>`, I want to `<action>` so that `<benefit>`.

## 5. Requirements
| ID | Requirement | Priority | Notes |
|----|-------------|----------|-------|
| R1 | <must-have> | P0 | |
| R2 | <should-have> | P1 | |
| R3 | <nice-to-have> | P2 | |

## 6. User Experience
> Flows, wireframes, or links to designs. Describe the happy path and key edge cases.

## 7. Success Metrics
| Metric | Baseline | Target | How measured |
|--------|----------|--------|--------------|
| <e.g. activation rate> | <x%> | <y%> | <source> |

## 8. Dependencies & Risks
- **Dependencies** — Teams, systems, or work this relies on.
- **Risks & mitigations** — What could go wrong and the plan for it.
- **Open questions** — Unresolved decisions.

## 9. Rollout Plan
- **Phasing** — MVP → fast-follow → full launch.
- **Launch criteria** — What must be true to ship.
- **Rollback plan** — How we revert if needed.

## 10. Timeline / Milestones
| Milestone | Owner | Target date |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| <Design complete> | | |
| <Eng complete> | | |
| <Launch> | | |

## 11. Appendix
> Links to research, related docs, prior art, and detailed specs.
